title: Jahmai Jones hits memorable first home run for Red Sox
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jahmai Jones hit his first home run for the Red Sox in a game against the Rays on July 18, 2026.
Jones's two-run homer tied the game 2-2, marking his second plate appearance for the Red Sox.
Traded from the Tigers, Jones hit all his MLB career home runs off left-handed pitchers.

title: Wilyer Abreu joins elite Red Sox company following his back-to-back multi-HR games
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Wilyer Abreu is the first Red Sox player since Mookie Betts in 2016 to hit back-to-back multi-home run games.
Abreu became the second Red Sox player to achieve consecutive multi-homer games at Fenway Park, following George Scott in 1971.
His performance helped Boston beat the Tampa Bay Rays and improve their record above .500, continuing a strong winning streak.

title: Red Sox extend season-altering win streak to 12 games in epic comeback fashion
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 12 games after defeating the Tampa Bay Rays in a comeback victory.
Wilyer Abreu hit his fourth home run in two games, contributing to the Red Sox overcoming a three-run deficit.
The Red Sox maintain the American League's final wild-card spot, one game ahead of the Seattle Mariners.

title: Report: LeBron James ‘hates’ Celtics, would rank them last on his list of teams to join
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James plans to announce his next NBA team soon, with the Celtics not in consideration.
ESPN's Brian Windhorst reports James ranks Boston 30th on his preferred teams list, citing strong dislike.
James criticizes Boston fans as racist, referencing negative experiences playing against the Celtics.

title: Tom Brady busy at Fanatics Fest, from a face slap to showing off his arm
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tom Brady slapped Logan Paul during a panel at Fanatics Fest in NYC, creating viral online buzz.
The slap stemmed from a long-standing playful feud, intensified by a flag football game loss earlier this year.
Besides the panel incident, Brady showcased his athletic skills by accurately throwing a baseball at the event.

title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men face kidnapping and assault charges following an attack near a playground on Thursday afternoon.
Victim was allegedly beaten and tied with an extension cord, sustaining injuries but none life-threatening, police report states.
Suspects identified as Calray Brown, Dijon Hazel, Newton Lawrence, and Alvin Barnett, captured in witness video, have been released on bail.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport during targeted operations.
Rosales, protected under DACA, was detained due to a 2017 removal order. Her renewal application is pending.
Attorney Todd Pomerleau claims ICE arrests at Logan Airport have increased with no criminal histories involved.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Interstellar' (2014) is recommended for its parallels with 'The Odyssey' through themes of time travel and familial quests. (Hulu, Paramount+)
'Lawrence of Arabia' (1962) mirrors 'The Odyssey' with themes of self-discovery and war, featuring Peter O'Toole. (Netflix)
'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' (2000) offers a humorous take on Homer's tale with George Clooney's character resembling Odysseus. (Criterion Channel)

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games by sweeping a doubleheader against the Tampa Bay Rays.
In Game 1, the Red Sox achieved a 10-0 victory with Jake Bennett allowing only one hit in six innings.
Game 2 saw a 5-3 win with the bullpen allowing no runs over 6.2 innings, boosting Boston's record to 48-48.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are making progress in their recovery; Suarez may start on July 21.
Tanner Houck is throwing off a mound twice a week, showing notable recovery from Tommy John surgery.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are expected to return this season, improving their rehabilitation status.
